title: Tableau Pulse Metrics API Import Integration
story: As a data analyst, I want to import metrics from Tableau Pulse into the DataTools Pro Metrics Glossary, so that I can maintain a unified view of metrics across both platforms and link directly to Tableau Pulse from my glossary.
description:
Adds an Import from Tableau Pulse feature to the Metrics Glossary. When the user clicks the import button, a modal opens. If Tableau API credentials are not yet configured, the user is prompted to complete a one-time setup.

### Authentication Setup
The user provides four fields: Tableau server URL, content URL (site name), Personal Access Token Name, and Personal Access Token Secret. These credentials are encrypted and stored. The server authenticates using the Tableau REST API sign-in endpoint (`/api/3.6/auth/signin`) with the personal access token credentials.

### Metric Import Flow
After authentication, the system fetches metric definitions from Tableau Pulse using the `/api/-/pulse/definitions` endpoint with `view=DEFINITION_VIEW_FULL`. Each returned definition includes metadata (name, description), specification (datasource, measure, time dimension), and associated metrics.

The user can then:
- Add a single metric to the glossary (saved with type `tableau_metric`)
- Ignore a metric
- Multi-select metrics via checkboxes for bulk add
- Merge a Pulse metric with an existing glossary entry, preserving the association between the Tableau metric and the DataTools Pro record

The integration enables launching directly from the Metrics Glossary into Tableau Pulse for the associated metric.
acceptance_criteria: Import button opens a modal with Tableau Pulse integration
First-time users see a setup modal with fields for server, content URL, token name, and token secret
Info icons with tooltips explain each credential field
Personal Access Token is encrypted before storage
Authenticated users see a list of Tableau Pulse metric definitions
User can add a single metric to the glossary with type tableau_metric
User can multi-select metrics via checkboxes for bulk add
User can merge a Pulse metric with an existing glossary entry
Exception handling covers invalid credentials with clear error messages
Pulse metric URL is linked as a custom link on the glossary entry
ux_changes: New Import from Tableau Pulse button on Metrics Glossary. Opens a modal with setup form (server, content URL, token name, token secret) on first use, and a metric list view on subsequent use. Supports add, ignore, multi-select, and merge actions.
middleware_changes: Server-side proxy to Tableau REST API for authentication and Pulse metric definition retrieval. Handles token exchange, session management, and metric data parsing.
data_model_changes: New tableau_metric type for glossary entries. Storage for encrypted Tableau API credentials (server, content URL, PAT name, PAT secret). Association field linking DataTools Pro metrics to Tableau Pulse definition IDs.
api_changes: Proxy endpoints for Tableau REST API authentication (POST sign-in) and Pulse metric definitions (GET definitions). Credential storage and retrieval endpoints.
implementation_notes: Tableau Pulse API uses a different URL pattern than the standard REST API — the definitions endpoint is `/api/-/pulse/definitions` (note the `-` instead of API version). Authentication uses Personal Access Tokens, not OAuth. The team encountered 404 errors initially because the Pulse API endpoints require a specific URL pattern. The implementation includes robust exception handling for invalid credentials and API errors.
comment_summary: Extended back-and-forth between Ryan and Waqar resolving Tableau API authentication issues. The Pulse API endpoint URL pattern differs from standard Tableau REST API. PAT authentication was tested via Postman. Waqar implemented add, ignore, multi-select, and merge functionality for imported metrics. Exception handling was emphasized.
